task: I’ve got a basement plumbing issue that needs a repair crew to come inside, and I want to make sure the job gets done without the work dragging all over the house. What should I have ready before they arrive, and what should I keep an eye on while they’re working?

turns.agent_response:
For a basement plumbing crew, I’d set it up so they can get in, do the wet work, and stay contained.

Before they arrive, have this ready:
- Clear a straight path from the entrance to the basement and to the work area
- Move anything stored in the basement away from the pipes, walls, and floor drain area
- Cover or remove items that could get dusted, splashed, or damaged
- Make sure they know where the main water shutoff is
- If needed, label or point out any valves, cleanouts, or problem spots
- Keep pets and kids out of the work zone
- Have good lighting available, or ask if they’re bringing their own
- Ask them to protect floors and stairs with drop cloths or runners
- Confirm where they can stage tools, materials, and any removed pipe or debris

While they’re working, keep an eye on:
- Whether they’re staying in the agreed area and not spreading into other rooms
- Any new leaks, damp spots, or water running where it shouldn’t
- Whether they’re using floor protection and cleaning up as they go
- Any unexpected cutting, drilling, or opening up of walls/floors
- Whether they’re explaining changes before doing extra work
- How they handle shutoff, drainage, and cleanup

A good rule: if they need to change the scope, stop and get it approved before they keep going.

When they’re done, do a quick walk-through:
- Check for leaks
- Run water and flush fixtures if relevant
- Look at all joints, fittings, and patched areas
- Make sure the area is cleaned up and access is restored
- Don’t pay until the agreed work is finished and looks right

If you want, I can turn this into a simple pre-arrival and walk-through checklist you can keep on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly right. But I’d be tighter about it.

Before they show:
- Main shutoff found and tested, not just “pointed out”
- Clear the path myself. No excuses for them tracking mud through the house
- Anything worth keeping moved out of the basement. Don’t leave temptations sitting there
- Write down the estimate, job scope, and any parts they say they need
- Keep the light good enough to see what they’re doing
- Have receipts and old paperwork handy if this is a repeat problem

While they work:
- Stay on site. Don’t let them wander off and leave a mess or an open line
- Watch every change to the scope. If they start talking extras, stop them and ask why
- Check for water at every stage, not just at the end
- Make sure they’re not tearing into more than they had to
- Confirm what was replaced before they pack up

After:
- Run the water yourself
- Check the bill against the estimate line by line
- Keep the paperwork

That’s the short version. If you want, I can make it into a plain checklist you can print and keep by the basement door.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that’s the tighter version I’d use too.

Here’s a plain checklist:

### Before they arrive
- Find the **main shutoff** and **test it**
- Clear your own path to the basement and work area
- Move anything you care about out of the basement
- Put down or ask for floor protection if needed
- Keep the lighting good enough to see the work clearly
- Have the **estimate, job scope, and any promised parts** in hand
- Keep old paperwork, receipts, and photos handy if this is a repeat issue
- Know who the **one contact person** is

### While they’re working
- Stay on site
- Watch for any new water, leaks, or damp spots
- Make sure they stay within the agreed work area
- Stop them if they start talking about extra work and ask why
- Don’t let them leave anything open, exposed, or half-secured
- Check that they’re not opening up more than they need to
- Ask what was replaced before they pack up

### After they finish
- Run the water yourself
- Check the repair area for leaks and proper cleanup
- Compare the bill to the estimate line by line
- Keep all paperwork, receipts, and photos

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page version with checkboxes.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the one.

Short, tight, no fluff. I’d use that.

Checklist is fine. One-page with boxes would be better, if you’re making it.
